info.title: Stopping the cluster services for the whole cluster

abstract.para: Stopping the cluster services means stopping &pace; and &corosync;. You can stop the whole cluster at once by using the --all option. In this mode, the cluster is no longer running and all the resources stop. Alternatively, you can avoid resource downtime by using this mode in combination with maintenance mode.

warning.title: Graceful shutdown not guaranteed
warning.para: When you stop the cluster services for the whole cluster without maintenance mode, all the resources stop. However, the resources must finish stopping before the cluster's shutdown timeout. If stopping a resource fails or times out, the resource's node is fenced.
If any resources take a long time to stop, consider stopping them before you stop the cluster services.

topic.para: You can perform this procedure on any node in the cluster.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
Check the status of the cluster:

listitem.para: If the resources are in maintenance mode, they will continue to run even while the cluster services are offline.
If the resources aren't in maintenance mode, they will all stop when you stop the cluster services.

step.para: Stop the cluster services on all nodes at once:
Check the status of the cluster services:
